riskFactors: Credit-pool transparency is limited: public official documentation does not disclose a verified loan book, borrower concentration, maturity ladder, default history, or tranche waterfall.
On-chain evidence is incomplete: DeFiLlama identifies Credix on Solana, but no verified public pool token or program address was confirmed from official docs in this pass.
Liquidity risk is high because no public DEX pairs, bid/ask spread, secondary-market depth, or redemption period were verified.
Legal-structure risk is elevated because public documents reviewed did not provide issuer vehicle, investor claim structure, custodian, trustee, or legal opinion details.
DeFiLlama shows active-loan exposure but negligible TVL, suggesting standard TVL may not reflect the economics of the credit book.
Credix public positioning appears to have shifted toward CrediPay B2B BNPL infrastructure, which may reduce comparability with older RWA protocol listings.
mitigants: The product category is economically relevant to RWA because it maps to B2B credit, receivables, and private-credit financing.
Official CrediPay documentation describes an operational credit lifecycle involving sellers, buyers, orders, invoices, repayments, and upfront seller settlement.
DeFiLlama still tracks Credix as an RWA Lending protocol and reports active loans, providing at least one external market-data reference point.
Credix maintains a public GitHub organization with historical Solana and application repositories, supporting technical traceability even though current contract evidence remains incomplete.
Using the credit_pool grading profile prevents inappropriate treasury-style reserve scoring and keeps missing pool-specific evidence visible.

_meta.notes: Conservative risk baseline pending official legal, pool, contract, and performance data.
